title: Quamoclit angulata
intro: Quamoclit angulata, also known as the Five-angled silver vine, is a type of flowering plant in the clematis family native to tropical Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of anxiety, insomnia, and digestive issues. Quamoclit angulata contains bioactive constituents such as flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are believed to contribute to its therapeutic effects. In herbal medicine, Quamoclit angulata is often prepared as a tea or tincture, and is also used in combination with other herbs to make sleep-promoting and anxiety-reducing supplements.
taxonomy: Quamoclit angulata is a species of flowering plant in the family Convolvulaceae. It is commonly known as the angled clematis or five-pointed clematis. As a member of the Convolvulaceae family, Quamoclit angulata is closely related to other flowering plants such as morning glories and bindweeds. Quamoclit angulata is native to tropical America and is often cultivated for its attractive flowers and versatile growth habits.
uses: Quamoclit angulata, also known as the five-angle calabar bean or scarlet glimezia, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes.
